title: verbascum × horticola
intro: Verbascum × horticola, also known as the garden mullein, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Verbascum genus and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis. The leaves and flowers of the Verbascum × horticola plant have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and promoting healthy digestion. Verbascum × horticola can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
